---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Andrew S. Rosenberg"
page-layout: article
toc: false
---

::: {.home-hero}
![](assets/headshot.webp){.headshot fig-alt="Andrew S. Rosenberg"}

# Andrew S. Rosenberg

::: {.title}
Associate Professor of Political Science · University of Florida
:::

::: {.social}
<a href="mailto:andrew.rosenberg@ufl.edu" aria-label="Email"><i class="bi bi-envelope"></i></a>
<a href="assets/asr-cv.pdf" aria-label="CV"><i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i></a>
<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=" aria-label="Google Scholar"><i class="bi bi-google"></i></a>
<a href="https://github.com/asrosenberg" aria-label="GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i></a>
:::
:::

::: {.home-section}

## About

I am an Associate Professor and Director of Graduate Studies in the [Department of Political Science](https://politicalscience.ufl.edu/) at the University of Florida. My research examines the intersection of race and racism in international politics, global inequality, migration policy, forced displacement, and international order. My work has appeared in the *American Journal of Political Science*, *Political Analysis*, *International Studies Quarterly*, *Journal of Peace Research*, and other journals.

My first book, [*Undesirable Immigrants: Why Racism Persists in International Migration*](books.qmd#undesirable-immigrants) (Princeton University Press, 2022), received the 2023 Best Book Award from the Race, Ethnicity, and Politics section of the American Political Science Association. I am writing a second book, *Hierarchies of Worth: Race and the Politics of Costly State Action*.

I received my Ph.D. from Ohio State University, an M.Sc. (Research) in International Relations from the London School of Economics, and a B.A. in International Studies and Political Science from Johns Hopkins University. I grew up in Des Moines, Iowa.

## Awards and Recognition

- **2023 Best Book Award**, Race, Ethnicity, and Politics section, American Political Science Association — for *Undesirable Immigrants*.
- **Affordable UF** badge for course materials under \$20 per credit hour.

## Recent Articles

::: {.pub-entry}
[Assessing Border Walls' Varied Impacts on Terrorist Group Diffusion]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg and Nazli Avdan]{.pub-authors}\
[Conflict Management and Peace Science, 2024]{.pub-venue}

[Examines how barriers affect the spread of violence among extremist organizations between 1970 and 2017 using network analysis.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1177/07388942241270927)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Race and Systemic Crises in International Politics: An Agenda for Pluralistic Scholarship]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg]{.pub-authors}\
[Review of International Studies 50(3): 457–475, 2024]{.pub-venue}

[Argues that racial inequality intersects with climate change, inequality, and migration in 21st-century international crises.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1017/S0260210523000761)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Where There's a Will, There's a Way: Border Walls and Refugees]{.pub-title}\
[Nazli Avdan, Andrew S. Rosenberg, and Christopher Gelpi]{.pub-authors}\
[Journal of Peace Research, 2023]{.pub-venue}

[Tests whether border fencing effectively reduces refugee flows between 1970 and 2017.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1177/00223433231200918)
:::
:::

[See all publications →](publications.qmd)

## Software

::: {.pub-entry}
[asymmetric]{.pub-title} — R package
[A toolkit for testing asymmetric hypotheses, accompanying Rosenberg, Knuppe, and Braumoeller (2017, *Political Analysis*).]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[GitHub](https://github.com/asrosenberg)
[Paper](https://doi.org/10.1017/pan.2017.16)
:::
:::

## Teaching

I teach graduate methods and IR theory at Florida. Recent and current courses include **Topics in Political Methodology (POS 6747)** (Spring 2026), **Linear Models**, **Political Network Analysis**, and a pre-term **Math Workshop**. Past undergraduate offerings include **Networks in International Relations** and **Data Visualization and Literacy**. Syllabi are available on request — see [Teaching →](teaching.qmd).

:::
